blockquote.p: Note to Balloters for Ballot5: Changes update Specimen's focus, collection, processing, container, search-parameter, example, and terminology surfaces for the current ballot window.
This resource is referenced from the Diagnostics Module; reviewers should also consider related module-page ballot content when reviewing this resource.

ul.li: Added Specimen.focus (CodeableReference) to identify the entity that is the focus of testing FHIR-53384
Specimen.processing.additive promoted from CodeableReference to a BackboneElement with new additive.type and additive.quantity, and container.device added for richer container detail FHIR-53723

Removed the FHIR-defined specimen-role ValueSet and CodeSystem and rebound the element to a THO (terminology.hl7.org) value set; an explanatory note was added to the introduction FHIR-55481, FHIR-55276
Added a value set binding for SpecimenDefinition.subject[x] (affects the sibling SpecimenDefinition resource) FHIR-54840
Refined the Specimen.status value set to avoid conflating background workflow status with the resource's own data status FHIR-54892
Corrected the binding mismatch on the collection device element; collection.device[x] replaced by collection.device FHIR-54488
Revised the narrative around nesting specimen containers FHIR-54598
Added narrative bullets describing how SpecimenDefinition relates to other resources (affects the sibling SpecimenDefinition resource) FHIR-54837
Additional changes as described in FHIR-56301 and FHIR-54843.
taken from a biological entity, living or dead
taken from a physical object or the environment

div.p: Some specimens are biological and can contain one or more components including but not limited to cellular molecules, cells, tissues, organs, body fluids, embryos, and body excretory products (source: NCI Thesaurus, modified).

The specimen resource covers substances used for diagnostic and environmental testing. The focus of the specimen resource is the process for gathering, maintaining and processing the specimen as well as where the specimen originated. This is distinct from the use of Substance which is only used when these other aspects are not relevant.

The current definition of the specimen resource contains only basic information about specimen containers. The recursive nature of container should be expressed via Specimen.device.parent. The frequency with which these elements are tracked may depend on the context of use; general lab, bio-banking, etc. Comments from reviewers on the appropriate scope for this resource, and the need for tracking related specimen management attributes, are welcomed.
